steps.title: Locate the boundary line
steps.content: The shaded region in the graph is bounded by a horizontal line at $y = 36$. A horizontal boundary tells us the inequality restricts the $y$-coordinate, not $x$.

steps.title: Determine the direction of shading
steps.content: The shaded region lies above the line $y = 36$, meaning every shaded point has a $y$-value greater than or equal to 36. Because the boundary itself is solid, equality is included.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A. $x \le 36$ — describes a vertical boundary, but the boundary here is horizontal. Eliminated.
  • B. $x \ge 36$ — also vertical-boundary; wrong variable. Eliminated.
  • C. $y \le 36$ — correct variable but would shade below the line, not above. Eliminated.
  • D. $y \ge 36$ — horizontal boundary at $y=36$ with shading above it. Matches the graph.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
